title: hypericum punctatum f. subpetiolatum
intro: Hypericum punctatum f. subpetiolatum, also known as spotted St. John's Wort or lesser St. John's Wort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the St. John's Wort family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which make it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Hypericum punctatum f. subpetiolatum include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, where it can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
